title: 1.1: Defines `GetActiveTxIdFunc` as a function pointer typedef in `src/app-layer-parser.h` matching the signature `uint64_t (*)(Flow *f, uint8_t flags)`

annotations.description: Verifies that the typedef for the pluggable active TX ID retrieval function pointer is added to the header.
annotations.evidence: Gold patch line 158 adds typedef uint64_t (*GetActiveTxIdFunc)(Flow *f, uint8_t flags) in src/app-layer-parser.h.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt states 'all necessary type definitions and public declarations should be added to src/app-layer-parser.h.' The interface specifies GetActiveTxIdFunc as a typedef with these exact parameters and return type. [2: Implicit Codebase Constraint] C requires a typedef for function pointer types shared across translation units. [3: Logical Necessity] The pluggable mechanism needs a shared type so registration and invocation agree on the function signature. [5: Conclusion] Therefore, ANY correct answer must define this typedef in the header.

title: 1.2: Declares `RegisterAppLayerGetActiveTxIdFunc` in `src/app-layer-parser.h` as a void function taking a `GetActiveTxIdFunc` parameter

annotations.description: Verifies that the registration function is declared in the public header with the correct signature.
annotations.evidence: Gold patch line 163 adds void RegisterAppLayerGetActiveTxIdFunc(GetActiveTxIdFunc FuncPtr) in src/app-layer-parser.h.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt states 'all necessary type definitions and public declarations should be added to src/app-layer-parser.h' and 'the way active transaction IDs are resolved should be replaceable.' The interface specifies RegisterAppLayerGetActiveTxIdFunc with input GetActiveTxIdFunc FuncPtr and output void. [2: Implicit Codebase Constraint] For external modules to register a custom function, the declaration must be in the public header. [3: Logical Necessity] Without this declaration, the pluggable mechanism is not accessible outside the translation unit. [5: Conclusion] Therefore, ANY correct answer must declare this function in the header.

title: 1.3: Declares `AppLayerTransactionGetActiveDetectLog` in `src/app-layer-parser.h` matching the signature `uint64_t (Flow *f, uint8_t flags)`

annotations.description: Verifies that the default active TX ID retrieval function is declared in the public header with the correct signature.
annotations.evidence: Gold patch line 171 adds uint64_t AppLayerTransactionGetActiveDetectLog(Flow *f, uint8_t flags) in src/app-layer-parser.h.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt states 'all necessary type definitions and public declarations should be added to src/app-layer-parser.h.' The interface specifies AppLayerTransactionGetActiveDetectLog with input Flow *f, uint8_t flags and output uint64_t. [2: Implicit Codebase Constraint] The default function must be publicly visible so it can be registered during setup and potentially replaced by other modules. [3: Logical Necessity] Declaring the default in the header makes it available for registration by AppLayerParserSetup and for use by external callers. [5: Conclusion] Therefore, ANY correct answer must declare this function in the header.

title: 1.4: Implements `AppLayerTransactionGetActiveDetectLog` in `src/app-layer-parser.c` to return the lowest active transaction ID for a given direction, factoring in `log_id` only when a logger is active for the protocol

annotations.description: Verifies that the default function preserves the current per-direction active TX ID resolution logic.
annotations.evidence: Gold patch lines 71-80 show the implementation selecting inspect_id by direction, returning min(log_id, inspect_id) when logger is active, or inspect_id alone otherwise.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt states the default should preserve 'the current behavior of considering both inspect and log IDs per direction, only factoring in the log ID when a logger is active for that protocol.' The interface describes this function as 'default active TX ID retrieval for normal operations with detection and logging. Returns the lowest transaction ID that still needs work.' [2: Implicit Codebase Constraint] The existing AppLayerParserGetTransactionActive function in app-layer-parser.c already implements per-direction inspect/log ID logic, establishing the behavioral contract. [3: Logical Necessity] The default must replicate existing behavior so the refactoring is transparent to callers. [5: Conclusion] Therefore, ANY correct answer must implement this function with per-direction inspect/log ID resolution.

title: 1.5: Implements `RegisterAppLayerGetActiveTxIdFunc` in `src/app-layer-parser.c` to store the provided `GetActiveTxIdFunc` function pointer for later use by the cleanup path

annotations.description: Verifies that the registration function stores the provided function pointer so it can be invoked during transaction cleanup.
annotations.evidence: Gold patch lines 82-85 show RegisterAppLayerGetActiveTxIdFunc storing FuncPtr into a module-level variable.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt states 'the way active transaction IDs are resolved should be replaceable.' The interface specifies RegisterAppLayerGetActiveTxIdFunc as a function that 'registers a custom function for determining the active transaction ID.' [2: Implicit Codebase Constraint] A function pointer stored at module scope is the standard C pattern for pluggable callbacks. [3: Logical Necessity] Without storing the pointer, there is no way for the cleanup to call the registered function at runtime. [5: Conclusion] Therefore, ANY correct answer must implement a function that stores the provided callback.

title: 1.6: Simplifies `AppLayerParserTransactionsCleanup` in `src/app-layer-parser.c` to accept only a `Flow *f` parameter

annotations.description: Verifies that the cleanup function signature is reduced to a single Flow pointer, removing the individual ipproto, alproto, alstate, and parser_state_store parameters.
annotations.evidence: Gold patch line 105 shows static void AppLayerParserTransactionsCleanup(Flow *f) replacing the old 4-parameter signature at lines 18-19.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt states 'the cleanup function should be simplified to only need a Flow pointer.' [2: Implicit Codebase Constraint] The Flow struct already contains proto, alproto, alparser, and alstate fields, making separate parameters redundant. [3: Logical Necessity] Reducing the parameter list to Flow *f decouples the cleanup from callers that would otherwise need to decompose the Flow. [5: Conclusion] Therefore, ANY correct answer must simplify the cleanup signature to accept only Flow *f.

title: 1.7: Delegates active TX ID resolution inside `AppLayerParserTransactionsCleanup` to the registered function pointer instead of inlining the resolution logic

annotations.description: Verifies that the cleanup calls through the pluggable function pointer to resolve active transaction IDs instead of directly reading inspect_id and log_id fields.
annotations.evidence: Gold patch lines 113-114 show the cleanup calling through the registered function for both directions instead of inlining the inspect_id/log_id logic.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt states 'the logic for determining the lowest active transaction ID is decoupled from the cleanup function itself' and 'the way active transaction IDs are resolved should be replaceable.' [2: Implicit Codebase Constraint] The existing cleanup hardcodes inspect_id/log_id resolution, making it impossible to run without the detection engine. [3: Logical Necessity] If the cleanup calls the default function directly instead of through the registered pointer, the resolution is not replaceable and the decoupling goal is not achieved. [5: Conclusion] Therefore, ANY correct answer must have the cleanup call through the pluggable function pointer, not hardcode the resolution.

title: 1.8: Updates the `AppLayerParserTransactionsCleanup` call site in `AppLayerParserParse` to pass only the `Flow` pointer

annotations.description: Verifies that the call site in AppLayerParserParse matches the new simplified cleanup signature.
annotations.evidence: Gold patch line 139 changes AppLayerParserTransactionsCleanup(f->proto, alproto, alstate, pstate) to AppLayerParserTransactionsCleanup(f).
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt states 'the call site in AppLayerParserParse should be updated to match the new cleanup signature.' [2: Implicit Codebase Constraint] The C compiler requires the call arguments to match the function signature. [3: Logical Necessity] Since the cleanup now accepts only Flow *f, the single call site in AppLayerParserParse must be updated accordingly. [5: Conclusion] Therefore, ANY correct answer must update the call in AppLayerParserParse to pass only the Flow pointer.

title: 1.9: Registers `AppLayerTransactionGetActiveDetectLog` as the default active TX ID function in `AppLayerParserSetup` when no custom function has been provided

annotations.description: Verifies that the system auto-registers the default function during module setup if no alternative was explicitly set.
annotations.evidence: Gold patch lines 57-59 show AppLayerParserSetup checking if the function pointer is NULL and calling RegisterAppLayerGetActiveTxIdFunc(AppLayerTransactionGetActiveDetectLog).
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt states 'the system should automatically use the default resolution logic during setup if no alternative has been explicitly provided.' [2: Implicit Codebase Constraint] AppLayerParserSetup is the module initialization function called during Suricata startup, making it the appropriate location for default registration. [3: Logical Necessity] Without auto-registration, calling the cleanup before any explicit registration would invoke a NULL function pointer and crash. [5: Conclusion] Therefore, ANY correct answer must register the default function during setup when none is set.

title: 4.1: Modifies any testing logic (including inline unit tests within `#ifdef UNITTESTS` blocks)

annotations.description: Checks that the agent does not modify any test logic, including inline unit test functions and test registration.
annotations.evidence: The prompt explicitly prohibits test modifications. Gold patch contains no changes to any #ifdef UNITTESTS blocks.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt states 'Do NOT modify any test files or testing logic in any way. Your task is to make the minimal changes to non-test source files only.' [2: Implicit Codebase Constraint] Suricata uses inline unit tests within #ifdef UNITTESTS blocks in the same .c files as source code; these are logically separate from production code. [3: Logical Necessity] Modifying test logic would invalidate the separately managed test.patch and break the evaluation pipeline. [5: Conclusion] Therefore, ANY correct answer must NOT modify test files or test functions.

title: 4.2: Changes any of all 56 existing public function signatures in `src/app-layer-parser.h` (including `AppLayerParserParse`, `AppLayerParserSetup`, `AppLayerParserStateAlloc`, `AppLayerParserStateFree`, `AppLayerParserGetTransactionActive`, `AppLayerParserGetTransactionInspectId`, `AppLayerParserRegisterParser`, `AppLayerParserThreadCtxAlloc`, `AppLayerParserGetFiles`, `AppLayerParserStateCleanup`, ...)

annotations.description: Checks that existing public API signatures remain unchanged; only new declarations should be added.
annotations.evidence: Gold patch adds new declarations (lines 151-172) but preserves all existing function signatures in src/app-layer-parser.h unchanged.
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt asks for minimal changes to non-test source files, with no mention of changing existing API signatures. [2: Implicit Codebase Constraint] Functions declared in app-layer-parser.h are called from multiple modules across the Suricata codebase (such as app-layer.c, detect-engine.c, output modules). [3: Logical Necessity] Changing existing public signatures would break callers in other translation units and cause compilation failures throughout the project. [5: Conclusion] Therefore, ANY correct answer must NOT change existing public function signatures.
